content: The hard working memory capacity limits that constrain an agent’s reasoning can be turned into a structural driver of more sample-efficient active learning, by using the unavoidable bottleneck of limited working memory as a natural curiosity signal that prioritizes which unobserved experiences to encode next. This creates an intrinsic reward framework where memory constraint itself guides active memory expansion, rather than requiring hand-designed curiosity modules like ICM or RND.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
